solutions.solution: Statement: Some pens are tables. No table is blue. Conclusion: I. Some tables are pens. II. No pen is blue.
Statement: All books are pencils. All pencils are flowers. Conclusion: I. All books are flowers. II. Some pencils are not books.
Statement: Some crows are jackals. No fox is a crow. Conclusion: I. Some jackals are foxes. II. Some jackals are not foxes.
Statement: Some crows are jackals. All foxes are crows. Conclusion: I. Some jackals are foxes. II. Some jackals are not foxes.
Statement: All radios are electric goods. All table-lamps are electric goods. Conclusion: I. Some radios are table-lamps. II. Some table-lamps are radios.
Statement: No man is a monkey. John is a man. Conclusion: I. John is not a monkey. II. John may or may not be a monkey.
Statement: A graduate is a man. This thief is a graduate. Conclusion: I. This thief is a man. II. Some men are thieves.
Statement: Only first divisioners are admitted. Ram is a first divisioner. Conclusion: I. Ram is admitted. II. Only Ram is admitted.
Statement: Men are sinners. Saints are men. Conclusion: I. Saints are sinners. II. Sinners are saints.
Statement: All businessmen except Bhimji are dishonest. All educated people smoke. Conclusion: I. All businessmen except Bhimji smoke. II. Bhimji smokes.
Statement: Only cats are animals. No historian is an animal.
